title: Commerce owner editor — extended fields + single COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN permission
summary: Collapse 10 per-section COMMERCE_*_EDIT_OWN permissions into one COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN, migrate all 30+ consumer files, add new owner-editable fields (type, summary, i18n, FAQs, experience price), and polish web editor (remove phantom website, add linkedIn).

tasks.title: Delete 10 per-section perms, add COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN to PermissionEnum
tasks.description:
In packages/schemas/src/enums/permission.enum.ts:
- Remove the 10 SPEC-239 owner-scoped entries (lines ~849-858): COMMERCE_SCHEDULE_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_CONTACT_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_SOCIAL_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_MEDIA_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_MENU_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_PRICE_RANGE_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_RICH_DESCRIPTION_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_AMENITIES_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_FEATURES_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN.
- Add COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN = 'commerce.editOwn' in the same block (before the admin-level group).
- Keep COMMERCE_CREATE, COMMERCE_VIEW_ALL, COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL, COMMERCE_DELETE, COMMERCE_MODERATE_REVIEW untouched.
Acceptance: enum compiles, 10 old values gone, 1 new value present, count of commerce.* entries drops from 15 to 6.

tasks.title: Rewrite permission-commerce.test.ts for single COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN
tasks.description:
In packages/schemas/src/enums/__tests__/permission-commerce.test.ts:
- Remove the 10 individual 'owner-scoped section edit permissions' it() blocks.
- Remove the 'should have exactly 15 new COMMERCE entries' count assertion (or update to 6).
- Add: it('should have COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN', () => expect(PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN).toBe('commerce.editOwn')).
- Add: it('should have exactly 6 COMMERCE entries', ...) covering COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN + the 5 admin-level ones.
- Covers AC-2 (enum side).
Acceptance: all tests pass, no reference to old per-section perms.

tasks.title: Update seed: replace 10 per-section perms with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN in COMMERCE_OWNER bundle
tasks.description:
In packages/seed/src/required/rolePermissions.seed.ts (lines ~958-978):
- Find the COMMERCE_OWNER role block.
- Replace the 10 PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_*_EDIT_OWN entries with a single PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
- Keep all other permissions in the block unchanged.
Acceptance: file compiles, no TypeScript errors on the removed enum members, seed runs without error (pnpm db:seed:test-users).

tasks.title: Rewrite commerce.permissions.ts: collapse COMMERCE_OWNER_EDIT_PERMISSIONS + checkCanEditOwn signature
tasks.description:
In packages/service-core/src/services/commerce/commerce.permissions.ts:
- Remove the COMMERCE_OWNER_EDIT_PERMISSIONS readonly array (was 10 entries).
- Rewrite checkCanEditOwn(actor, entity, ownSectionPermission) → checkCanEditOwn(actor, entity): the ownSectionPermission parameter is eliminated because all sections now share one permission (COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN).
  New logic: allow if COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL OR (COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN AND isOwner).
- Rewrite checkCanEditOwnOrAll(actor, entity): allow if COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL OR (COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN AND isOwner). Remove the COMMERCE_OWNER_EDIT_PERMISSIONS.some() fan-out.
- Update JSDoc on all modified functions.
- Update the exports list in index.ts if needed.
Acceptance: compiles, no reference to removed per-section perms.

tasks.title: Rewrite gastronomy.permissions.ts: drop checkGastronomyCanEditOwn section param, update checkGastronomyCanEditFaqs
tasks.description:
In packages/service-core/src/services/gastronomy/gastronomy.permissions.ts:
- checkGastronomyCanEditOwn: remove ownSectionPermission parameter (was PermissionEnum); delegate to updated checkCanEditOwn(actor, entity) (no third arg).
- checkGastronomyCanEditFaqs: change the call from checkCanEditOwn(actor, entity, PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN) to checkCanEditOwn(actor, entity) — now gates on COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
- Update JSDoc.
Acceptance: compiles, no per-section perm references.

tasks.title: Rewrite gastronomy.service.ts updateOwn: single COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN gate, add new fields
tasks.description:
In packages/service-core/src/services/gastronomy/gastronomy.service.ts, updateOwn():
- Replace the 9-section per-field permission fan-out block with a single check:
  if (!hasEditAll) { checkGastronomyCanEditOwn(actor, entity); } (one call, no section routing).
- Extend the validated payload to include: type, summary, nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n (once T-013 adds them to GastronomyOwnerUpdateInputSchema).
- Update JSDoc to reflect collapsed gating model.
- Keep ownership check (NOT_FOUND for non-owners) and EDIT_ALL bypass.
Acceptance: compiles, updateOwn still delegates to this.update(), per-section checks gone.

tasks.title: Rewrite experience.permissions.ts + experience.service.ts updateOwn parallel to gastronomy
tasks.description:
Mirror T-005 and T-006 for the experience vertical:
- packages/service-core/src/services/experience/experience.permissions.ts:
  Same changes as T-005: drop ownSectionPermission param from checkExperienceCanEditOwn, update checkExperienceCanEditFaqs.
- packages/service-core/src/services/experience/experience.service.ts, updateOwn():
  Same changes as T-006: single checkExperienceCanEditOwn(actor, entity) gate, remove section fan-out. Also extend accepted fields: type, summary, nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n, priceFrom, priceUnit (once T-014 adds them to ExperienceOwnerUpdateInputSchema).
Acceptance: compiles, no per-section perm references.

tasks.title: Update API FAQ routes (gastronomy + experience protected): COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN → COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN
tasks.description:
The 8 protected FAQ route files reference COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN in their JSDoc/comments (no runtime import of the enum — gating is done inside the service via checkGastronomyCanEditFaqs / checkExperienceCanEditFaqs). Update each file's header comment to reference COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
Files:
- apps/api/src/routes/gastronomy/protected/addFaq.ts
- apps/api/src/routes/gastronomy/protected/removeFaq.ts
- apps/api/src/routes/gastronomy/protected/reorderFaqs.ts
- apps/api/src/routes/gastronomy/protected/updateFaq.ts
- apps/api/src/routes/experience/protected/addFaq.ts
- apps/api/src/routes/experience/protected/removeFaq.ts
- apps/api/src/routes/experience/protected/reorderFaqs.ts
- apps/api/src/routes/experience/protected/updateFaq.ts
If any route directly imports COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN for a runtime check (verify), replace with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
Acceptance: no TypeScript errors, comments are accurate.

tasks.title: Rewrite service-core permission tests: commerce.permissions.test.ts, gastronomy.permissions.test.ts, experience.permissions.test.ts
tasks.description:
Update three test files after T-004/T-005/T-007:

packages/service-core/test/services/commerce/commerce.permissions.test.ts:
- Remove tests for checkCanEditOwn with per-section permission params.
- Add tests: checkCanEditOwn allows owner+COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN, denies owner+no-perm, allows staff+COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL.
- Add tests: checkCanEditOwnOrAll allows owner+COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN, denies stranger.

packages/service-core/test/services/gastronomy/gastronomy.permissions.test.ts:
- Remove per-section gating tests.
- Add COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN variants for checkGastronomyCanEditOwn and checkGastronomyCanEditFaqs.

packages/service-core/test/services/experience/experience.permissions.test.ts:
- Same as gastronomy.

Each test file should cover: allow-own, deny-stranger, staff-bypass for the updated helpers. Covers AC-2 at the service level.

tasks.title: Update gastronomy.service.test.ts and experience.service.test.ts: per-section permission blocks + T-022 regression
tasks.description:
packages/service-core/test/services/gastronomy/gastronomy.service.test.ts:
- Remove per-section permission test blocks (COMMERCE_SCHEDULE_EDIT_OWN etc. test scenarios in updateOwn).
- Add: owner with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN can call updateOwn for any field group.
- Update AC-3 identity-field regression (T-022 block, lines ~264-308): remove 'type' from the expected-stripped list (it is now owner-editable per AC-5 / D1). Verify 'name', 'slug', 'destinationId', lifecycle/visibility/moderation/isFeatured/ownerId still get stripped.
- Add new positive test: 'type' in the payload reaches this.update() (is NOT stripped).

packages/service-core/test/services/experience/experience.service.test.ts:
- Same AC-3/T-022 update: remove 'type' from forbidden list, add positive assertion.
- Remove per-section perm blocks.

Covers AC-5.

tasks.title: Update media permission gate: COMMERCE_MEDIA_EDIT_OWN → COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN in permissions.ts
tasks.description:
In apps/api/src/routes/media/admin/permissions.ts:
- In ENTITY_UPDATE_PERMISSIONS, replace PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_MEDIA_EDIT_OWN with PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN for both gastronomy and experience entries.
- The OWN_ANY_ENTITIES set and anyPermission logic (COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL bypass) stay unchanged.
Acceptance: compiles, no TypeScript errors from the removed enum member.

tasks.title: Update media permission-gate.test.ts: replace COMMERCE_MEDIA_EDIT_OWN with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN
tasks.description:
In apps/api/test/routes/media/permission-gate.test.ts, the 'Commerce media permissions (SPEC-249 T-015b)' block (lines ~307-350):
- Replace every occurrence of PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_MEDIA_EDIT_OWN with PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
- Test names and logic stay the same (allow owner+COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN, deny non-owner+COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN, deny no-perm).
- No new test cases needed; just the enum swap.
Covers AC-2 (media gate side).

tasks.title: Update GastronomyOwnerUpdateInputSchema: add type, summary, nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n
tasks.description:
In packages/schemas/src/entities/gastronomy/gastronomy.crud.schema.ts, GastronomyOwnerUpdateInputSchema:
- Add to the .pick() call: type (GastronomyTypeEnum), summary, nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n.
- These are all already on GastronomySchema via CommerceIdentityFields and the vertical-specific type field.
- Keep the existing .partial() so all are optional on the PATCH.
- Remove 'type' from the comment listing identity fields NOT permitted for owner.
- Update JSDoc to reflect the new permitted set.
- Verify the gastronomy.crud.schema.ts file stays under 500 lines; split if needed.
Acceptance: ExperienceOwnerUpdateInput type now includes the new fields; compiles; existing fields unchanged.

tasks.title: Update ExperienceOwnerUpdateInputSchema: add type, summary, i18n fields, priceFrom, priceUnit
tasks.description:
In packages/schemas/src/entities/experience/experience.crud.schema.ts, ExperienceOwnerUpdateInputSchema:
- Add to the .pick() call: type (ExperienceTypeEnum), summary, nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n, priceFrom, priceUnit.
- priceFrom is a z.number().nonnegative().nullish() (matches ExperienceSchema); priceUnit is ExperiencePriceUnitEnum nullish.
- Remove 'type', 'priceFrom', 'priceUnit' from the comment block that lists NOT permitted identity fields.
- Update JSDoc.
- Remove SPEC-249 T-022 AC-3 comment that placed 'type'/'priceFrom'/'priceUnit' in the identity-strip category (now owner-editable per D1).
Acceptance: ExperienceOwnerUpdateInput type includes the new fields; compiles.

tasks.title: Update admin commerceSections.ts: replace all per-section COMMERCE_*_EDIT_OWN with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN
tasks.description:
In apps/admin/src/features/commerce/config/commerceSections.ts:
- In createCommerceOperationalSection(), replace every occurrence of PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_CONTACT_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_SOCIAL_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_MEDIA_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_AMENITIES_EDIT_OWN, COMMERCE_FEATURES_EDIT_OWN (in section.permissions.edit and all field.permissions.edit arrays) with PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
- In createCommerceIdentitySection(), the richDescription field.permissions.edit references COMMERCE_RICH_DESCRIPTION_EDIT_OWN — replace with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN (or COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL if richDescription stays admin-only in the admin panel; confirm against spec §5 which keeps admin panel UI out of scope but still requires the enum migration).
- Update the file JSDoc comment that lists the per-section perm names.
Acceptance: compiles, no references to deleted enum members.

tasks.title: Update admin consolidated config tests for gastronomy and experience + JSDoc/docs cleanup
tasks.description:
In apps/admin/src/features/gastronomy/__tests__/gastronomy-consolidated.config.test.ts and apps/admin/src/features/experience/__tests__/experience-consolidated.config.test.ts:
- If any test asserts specific permission enum values on the operational section fields (e.g. COMMERCE_CONTACT_EDIT_OWN), replace those assertions with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
- If tests pass no permission assertions currently (grep showed 0 hits), verify that the test still compiles after the enum change — the config pulls from the updated commerceSections.ts.
- Add a smoke test asserting the operational section's edit permissions array includes COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
Acceptance: tests compile and pass.
Also includes: JSDoc/comments in gastronomy.faq.ts, experience.faq.ts, experience.crud.schema.ts, and apps/api/docs/route-architecture.md updated to reference COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.

tasks.title: Update gastronomy.faq.test.ts and experience.faq.test.ts: replace COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN
tasks.description:
In packages/service-core/test/services/gastronomy/gastronomy.faq.test.ts and packages/service-core/test/services/experience/experience.faq.test.ts:
- Find all test actors that carry PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN in their permissions array and replace with PermissionEnum.COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
- Ensure test descriptions still accurately reflect gating semantics.
- If an existing test checks that an actor WITHOUT COMMERCE_FAQS_EDIT_OWN is denied, update to check WITHOUT COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
Acceptance: tests compile and pass against the updated service helpers.

tasks.title: Update base-commerce-listing.service.test.ts: remove per-section perm references
tasks.description:
In packages/service-core/test/services/commerce/base-commerce-listing.service.test.ts:
- Find and replace all actor setups that use per-section COMMERCE_*_EDIT_OWN permissions.
- Replace with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN where the test is exercising owner-edit access.
- Add a 'should deny owner without COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN' negative test if not already present.
Acceptance: compiles, all tests pass.

tasks.title: Update spec-239 integration test: replace per-section perms with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN
tasks.description:
In packages/service-core/test/integration/services/spec-239-gastronomy-commerce.integration.test.ts:
- Replace actor setups and assertions that reference per-section COMMERCE_*_EDIT_OWN perms with COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN.
- Ensure the integration test still exercises the full updateOwn flow (owner can edit all field groups with single perm, staff still passes with COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL).
Acceptance: compiles and passes.

tasks.title: Add type select and summary textarea to CommerceListingEditor web component
tasks.description:
In apps/web/src/components/commerce/CommerceListingEditor.client.tsx:
- Add a controlled select input for 'type': uses the GastronomyTypeEnum or ExperienceTypeEnum depending on the 'vertical' prop. Render the correct enum options per vertical. Wires to the PATCH payload as { type: ... }.
- Add a controlled textarea for 'summary' (10-300 chars). Wires to the PATCH payload as { summary: ... }.
- Both fields go in a new 'Información básica' section above the existing contact group.
- Add the fields to the dirty tracking logic (markDirty('type'), markDirty('summary')).
- Add them to the PATCH payload builder.
- Update ContactValues / type interfaces as needed.
- Polish: remove the phantom 'website' field from the ContactValues interface and from the rendered contact form inputs (AC-4). The owner ContactInfoSchema does not include website.
- Add 'linkedIn' to SocialValues and SOCIAL_KEYS, add its text input in the social group (AC-4).
Covers AC-1 (type, summary), AC-4 (website removal, linkedIn addition).

tasks.title: Add experience priceFrom + priceUnit fields to CommerceListingEditor
tasks.description:
In apps/web/src/components/commerce/CommerceListingEditor.client.tsx:
- When vertical === 'experience', render a 'Precio' subsection with:
  - A number input for priceFrom (label 'Precio desde', min 0, nullish).
  - A select for priceUnit (ExperiencePriceUnitEnum values).
- Both are behind a vertical === 'experience' conditional so gastronomy editors don't see them.
- Wire to dirty tracking (markDirty('priceFrom'), markDirty('priceUnit')) and to the PATCH payload.
- The existing isPriceOnRequest toggle (boolean) stays for experience; priceFrom/priceUnit are additional (not exclusive).
Covers AC-1 (experience priceFrom/priceUnit).

tasks.title: Add CommerceTranslationPanel component (replicating SPEC-212 TranslationPanel pattern)
tasks.description:
Create apps/web/src/components/commerce/CommerceTranslationPanel.client.tsx (and matching .module.css):
- Replicate the SPEC-212 TranslationPanel pattern from apps/web/src/components/host/editor/TranslationPanel.client.tsx.
- Renders a locale-tab UI (es / en / pt) for 4 i18n fields: nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n.
- Props: initialI18n: { nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n } (all nullish), onChange: (field, locale, value) => void.
- Each locale tab contains: text input for nameI18n[locale], textarea for summaryI18n[locale], textarea for descriptionI18n[locale], textarea for richDescriptionI18n[locale].
- Uses vanilla CSS + CSS Modules (no Tailwind — web convention).
- Named export only.
Covers AC-3.

tasks.title: Wire CommerceTranslationPanel into CommerceListingEditor
tasks.description:
In apps/web/src/components/commerce/CommerceListingEditor.client.tsx:
- Import and render CommerceTranslationPanel below the basic-info section.
- Initialize with initialData.nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n from the listing's current payload.
- On onChange: merge the updated i18n object into state and mark dirty (e.g. markDirty('nameI18n')).
- On save: include each dirty i18n field in the PATCH payload as top-level keys (nameI18n: { es: ..., en: ..., pt: ... }).
- Use the existing CommerceListingDetail type — verify it exposes the 4 i18n fields from the protected getById endpoint; if not, update apps/web/src/lib/commerce/owner-listings.ts to include them.
Covers AC-1 + AC-3.

tasks.title: Add CommerceFaqManager component (web owner FAQ UI)
tasks.description:
Create apps/web/src/components/commerce/CommerceFaqManager.client.tsx:
- Renders a list of existing FAQs (question + answer rows).
- Add FAQ button → inline form (question textarea, answer textarea, submit).
- Edit FAQ: click row to expand inline edit form.
- Remove FAQ: confirmation step, calls DELETE endpoint.
- Reorder FAQs: up/down arrows or drag (minimal: up/down buttons suffice).
- All 4 operations wire to the existing protected FAQ endpoints:
  POST   /{vertical}/protected/:id/faqs (addFaq)
  PATCH  /{vertical}/protected/:id/faqs/:faqId (updateFaq)
  DELETE /{vertical}/protected/:id/faqs/:faqId (removeFaq)
  PUT    /{vertical}/protected/:id/faqs/reorder (reorderFaqs)
- Uses apiClient from @/lib/api/client.
- Props: listingId, vertical, locale, initialFaqs.
- Vanilla CSS + CSS Modules, named export.
Covers AC-1 (FAQ management).

tasks.title: Integrate CommerceFaqManager and CommerceTranslationPanel into editar.astro page
tasks.description:
In apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/mi-cuenta/comercio/[vertical]/[id]/editar.astro:
- Import CommerceFaqManager and pass it initialFaqs fetched SSR from the protected listFaqs endpoint (if available), or let it start empty and load on mount.
- Import CommerceTranslationPanel (already wired via T-023 into CommerceListingEditor — verify it renders in the page; if CommerceListingEditor already renders it, no separate wiring needed here).
- Verify the editar.astro page passes the new initialData fields (type, summary, nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n, priceFrom, priceUnit for experience) down to CommerceListingEditor via the CommerceListingDetail type.
- If CommerceListingDetail in apps/web/src/lib/commerce/owner-listings.ts is missing the new fields, extend the type here.
Covers AC-1 (wiring all new fields to the rendered page).

tasks.title: Write unit tests for CommerceTranslationPanel and CommerceFaqManager
tasks.description:
Create test files alongside or in a test/ dir near the components:
- apps/web/src/components/commerce/CommerceTranslationPanel.test.tsx:
  Test: renders 3 locale tabs (es/en/pt); switching tabs shows correct inputs; onChange fires with correct (field, locale, value); initial values are populated.
- apps/web/src/components/commerce/CommerceFaqManager.test.tsx:
  Test: renders initial FAQ list; Add FAQ form submits and calls addFaq endpoint mock; Remove FAQ shows confirmation; Reorder calls reorderFaqs endpoint.
  Use vitest + @testing-library/react.
Covers AC-3 (TranslationPanel UX) and AC-1 (FAQ manager behavior).

tasks.title: Write schema unit tests: GastronomyOwnerUpdateInputSchema and ExperienceOwnerUpdateInputSchema new fields
tasks.description:
In packages/schemas/src/entities/gastronomy/__tests__/ (create if absent) and packages/schemas/src/entities/experience/__tests__/:
- Test that type, summary, nameI18n, summaryI18n, descriptionI18n, richDescriptionI18n are accepted as valid optional fields.
- Test that name, slug, destinationId, ownerId, lifecycleStatus, visibility, moderationStatus, isFeatured are still rejected / stripped (not in the schema).
- For experience: test priceFrom (number, nonneg) and priceUnit (ExperiencePriceUnitEnum) are accepted.
- For gastronomy: test priceFrom, priceUnit are NOT in the schema (not applicable to gastronomy).
Covers AC-5 (identity-strip) and AC-1 (new fields accepted).

tasks.title: Write E2E test: owner can edit type + i18n fields and changes persist on public ficha (SPEC-252 harness)
tasks.description:
Using the SPEC-252 Playwright E2E harness (apps/e2e/):
- Sign in as a commerce owner (julieta@local.test or equivalent from SPEC-252 seed).
- Navigate to /[lang]/mi-cuenta/comercio/[vertical]/[id]/editar.
- Change the type select to a different value.
- Fill in the Spanish name in the TranslationPanel (es tab, nameI18n.es).
- Save the form.
- Navigate to the public ficha URL.
- Assert: the type badge reflects the new value; the es locale nameI18n is displayed.
Tag: @p0. Covers AC-1 (persist + appear on public ficha).

tasks.title: Write E2E test: owner without COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN gets FORBIDDEN on PATCH updateOwn
tasks.description:
Using the SPEC-252 Playwright E2E harness or direct API assertion:
- Use a USER-role actor (no COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN) to call PATCH /{vertical}/protected/:id (updateOwn endpoint) directly via fetch.
- Assert HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN response.
- Also assert: a COMMERCE_OWNER actor WITH COMMERCE_EDIT_OWN gets HTTP 200.
- Assert: a staff actor with COMMERCE_EDIT_ALL gets HTTP 200.
Tag: @p0. Covers AC-2 (permission gate).

tasks.title: Write E2E test: contact has no website field; social includes linkedIn
tasks.description:
Using the SPEC-252 Playwright E2E harness:
- Navigate to the owner editor for a commerce listing.
- Assert: no input with label/aria-label 'Sitio Web' or id 'website' in the contact section.
- Assert: an input for 'LinkedIn' is present in the social section and can be filled.
- Save with a linkedIn value; reload; assert the value persists.
Tag: @p0. Covers AC-4.

tasks.title: Final typecheck + full test run across affected packages
tasks.description:
Run across all affected packages to confirm zero regressions:
- pnpm typecheck (packages/schemas, packages/service-core, apps/api, apps/admin, apps/web)
- pnpm test (vitest, all affected packages)
- pnpm lint
Fix any remaining TypeScript errors from the enum removal (the dist/ files will be stale — ensure a fresh build of packages/schemas before running dependent package tests).
Block: all prior tasks (T-001..T-030) should be complete.
Acceptance: zero TS errors, zero test failures, biome lint clean.
